acceptance_targets: [CH08_AWARD_01_OVEREXPLAINED_TURN] After revising snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:111-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:149-159, a cold reader can point to one clear beat where the RT document changes Cliff's plan, and every paragraph after the photos adds new tactical information rather than restating that Tommy is exposed. If the closing material still mainly rephrases leverage or blackmail in cleaner language, this finding remains unresolved.

[CH08_CRAFT_01_LAW_OFFICE_ENVELOPE_DODGE] In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:65-69, a cold reader no longer expects Cliff to open the law-office envelope and feels the text has answered why he does not. Pass only if the page either shows inspection or supplies a specific on-page reason he cannot or will not inspect it; fail if he again shrugs it off as probable estate paper and simply puts it back.

[CH08_PROSE_01_CLIPPED_FRAGMENT_TIC] Across revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:47-59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:113-133, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:147-149, no cited passage contains a run of two or more bare noun-phrase sentences in a row. Cliff's observational rhythm should remain brisk, but the prose should no longer depend on stacked article-less fragments as its default move.

[FIRE_DOC_CONTRADICTIONS_chapter_08] Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2070; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2095 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.

[SAFE_CONTENTS_PHYSICAL_chapter_08] Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2055; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2070 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.
[R01] Ch8 mirror passage no longer names silver hair, permanent tan, or collared polo. New financial-stress details survive.
[R07] Ch8 opening does not re-describe A-frame shape, wraparound deck layout, pines, or lake frontage.
[R15] Ch8 does not use 'insurance,' 'claims,' or 'adjuster' as a metaphor for Paul's behavior.
[C03] Cliff's car is the same make in Ch1 and Ch8.
[C08] The RT envelope has a consistent size and seal state across Ch8 and Ch11.
[C11] The day the fire occurs is consistent between the two renderings of Martin's document. Tommy's memory in Ch12 either matches or is flagged as uncertain.
[length_chapter_08_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: After revising snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:111-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:149-159, a cold reader can point to one clear beat where the RT document changes Cliff's plan, and every paragraph after the photos adds new tactical information rather than restating that Tommy is exposed. If the closing material still mainly rephrases leverage or blackmail in cleaner language, this finding remains unresolved.
findings.problem: The chapter's major turn lands when Cliff finishes the RT letter and photographs it, but the back half keeps translating that same discovery into cleaner thematic language instead of sharpening the consequence. 'That was the leverage' and 'it was a restructuring' explain the turn once, then the closing paragraphs re-explain Tommy's exposure before arriving at the modest decision to wait. The reveal's temperature change is stated twice rather than converted into a tighter, more irreversible chapter ending.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the post-document sections at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:111-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:149-159. Keep the must-land beats of Cliff rereading the pages, photographing them, and revising from an immediate approach to a delayed one, but replace the abstract paraphrase of leverage with one concrete tactical consequence or cost that Cliff can observe now. End on the revised tactic or its risk, not on a second thematic summary of what the document means.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:65-69, a cold reader no longer expects Cliff to open the law-office envelope and feels the text has answered why he does not. Pass only if the page either shows inspection or supplies a specific on-page reason he cannot or will not inspect it; fail if he again shrugs it off as probable estate paper and simply puts it back.
findings.problem: Cliff's explicit objective is to find paperwork that can turn Martin's forty-thousand-dollar debt into an estate obligation, yet when he encounters a law-office envelope in the safe he dismisses it unopened. In a search motivated by enforceable paper trail and estate leverage, ignoring the one item most likely to contain legal instructions reads as authorial withholding rather than Cliff's actual behavior.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:65-69, either have Cliff inspect the law-office envelope and reject it for a page-specific reason, or give him a concrete obstacle that makes opening it impossible or strategically unwise right then. The revision must make his choice arise from fear, interruption, labeling, or another visible local pressure, not from the chapter needing the envelope untouched.

findings.acceptance_test: Across revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:47-59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:113-133, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:147-149, no cited passage contains a run of two or more bare noun-phrase sentences in a row. Cliff's observational rhythm should remain brisk, but the prose should no longer depend on stacked article-less fragments as its default move.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into clipped noun-phrase narration such as 'Printer ink, paper dust,' 'Birth certificate. Passport, expired,' 'Filing cabinet. Bookshelves,' 'Mirror above the sink,' and 'Pine sap. Lake water.' Used this often, the device stops reading like Cliff's fast inventorying and starts reading like a mechanical fragment scaffold. The recurrence is chapter-wide and also violates the constitution's anti-clipping rule for narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the fragment clusters at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:47-59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:113-133, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:147-149 into mostly full sentences that still preserve Cliff's inventorying speed and deal-brain specificity. One isolated fragment can still land as emphasis, but these spans need syntactic variety and complete sentences carrying both image and attitude.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2070; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2095 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.

acceptance_test.excerpt: 2070: The pages landed on the desk. The room was the same room. Filing cabinet. Bookshelves. Tom Clancy hardcovers. The lake print leaning against the wall. Water ran in the kitchen sink. A loon called from somewhere out on Elbow Lake.

evidence.excerpt: 2070: The pages landed on the desk. The room was the same room. Filing cabinet. Bookshelves. Tom Clancy hardcovers. The lake print leaning against the wall. Water ran in the kitchen sink. A loon called from somewhere out on Elbow Lake.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2070: The pages landed on the desk. The room was the same room. Filing cabinet. Bookshelves. Tom Clancy hardcovers. The lake print leaning against the wall. Water ran in the kitchen sink. A loon called from somewhere out on Elbow Lake.

findings.problem: Cliff reads the RT document: dated Aug 17 2002, four pages, kerosene heater knocked over in cabin kitchen, Toomey in back bedroom, 'friend of Tommy's uncle named Gunderson.'
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2070; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2095. Establish one canonical version of the 2002 fire (date, accelerant, structure, victim location, document page count) and enforce it wherever the RT document is read on-page (chapters 8 and 16 must match exactly). Tommy's memory (chapter 12) and confession (chapter 17) may diverge from the document if the divergence is intentional and flagged as unreliable narration, but the two on-page readings of the same physical document must be identical.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2055; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2070 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.

acceptance_test.excerpt: 2070: The pages landed on the desk. The room was the same room. Filing cabinet. Bookshelves. Tom Clancy hardcovers. The lake print leaning against the wall. Water ran in the kitchen sink. A loon called from somewhere out on Elbow Lake.

evidence.excerpt: 2070: The pages landed on the desk. The room was the same room. Filing cabinet. Bookshelves. Tom Clancy hardcovers. The lake print leaning against the wall. Water ran in the kitchen sink. A loon called from somewhere out on Elbow Lake.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2070: The pages landed on the desk. The room was the same room. Filing cabinet. Bookshelves. Tom Clancy hardcovers. The lake print leaning against the wall. Water ran in the kitchen sink. A loon called from somewhere out on Elbow Lake.

findings.problem: Cliff opens the safe: cash approximately $2,000, 'legal-size manila envelope,' document described as 'four pages' and later 'four sheets, eight images.'
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2055; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2070. Fix one canonical description of the safe contents (cash amount, envelope size and color, document page count) and use it in every scene where the safe is opened. If cash is removed between scenes, show it on-page or reference it. The envelope and document are the same physical objects and must match across all observations.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 mirror passage no longer names silver hair, permanent tan, or collared polo. New financial-stress details survive.
findings.evidence: Ch1 line 45 establishes Cliff: 'Silver hair, permanent tan, the kind of posture that made you aware of your own' in 'pressed chinos and a collared polo.' Ch8 line 2090 re-introduces all three traits in a mirror scene: 'Silver hair he paid sixty bucks to keep neat at a barber in Edina. Permanent tan from property lines and half-built decks. Collared polo bought on a maxed-out credit card.'
findings.problem: Ch8 line 2090 delivers a near-complete re-introduction of every physical trait from Ch1/45 (silver hair, permanent tan, collared polo) seven chapters later. First establishment: Ch1 line 45.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or heavily compress the mirror passage. Retain only new information (Tag Heuer, maxed credit card, gap between self-image and reality). Let one brief sensory detail carry appearance rather than re-cataloging all three Ch1 traits.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 opening does not re-describe A-frame shape, wraparound deck layout, pines, or lake frontage.
findings.evidence: Ch1/9 establishes the lake house: 'an A-frame on Elbow Lake, pine-paneled, with a deck wrapping around the lake side.' Ch8/1960 re-inventories: 'Cedar siding, original but maintained. Wraparound deck... Vaulted A-frame ceiling... Lake frontage, eighty feet, mature pines and birch, dock in decent shape.'
findings.problem: Ch8 opening (line 1960) re-describes the entire lake house from scratch through Cliff's appraisal lens: A-frame, wraparound deck, pines, birch, dock, fireplace, lake frontage. Every element was established in Ch1.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep Cliff's appraisal compulsion but appraise what is NEW or deteriorating — the deferred maintenance, the gap between value and what Cliff needs. Cut the re-catalog of A-frame, pines, lake frontage, and fireplace.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 does not use 'insurance,' 'claims,' or 'adjuster' as a metaphor for Paul's behavior.
findings.evidence: Paul's insurance identity is invoked 22 times across 10 chapters. Ch4/1024: 'insurance-adjuster precision.' Ch5/1327: self-identification in interview (functional). Ch7/1805: 'that insurance face.' Ch8/1970: 'Insurance claims, probably. Paul adjusted claims the way other people bit their nails.' Ch7 and Ch8 are consecutive chapters both invoking insurance as a character shorthand.
findings.problem: Paul's insurance/claims-adjuster identity is re-proved via metaphor in Ch8/1970 ('adjusted claims the way other people bit their nails') one chapter after the same metaphor pattern in Ch7/1805 ('that insurance face'). The adjacent-chapter cluster re-proves the baseline without new leverage.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch8/1970, describe Paul's behavior at the table without the insurance metaphor. Let the reader recognize Paul's precision from prior chapters rather than being re-told its source.

findings.acceptance_test: Cliff's car is the same make in Ch1 and Ch8.
findings.evidence: Ch1/7: 'Nate parked behind Cliff's Audi. The Audi was new, or new-looking.' Ch8/2004: 'Cliff's Lexus. The Lexus was leased. Fourteen hundred a month.'
findings.problem: Cliff's vehicle is an Audi in Ch1/7 and a Lexus in Ch8/2004. These are different makes. Only one car was driven to the lake house.
findings.rewrite_direction: Pick one make and correct the other. Given Cliff's financial stress narrative in Ch8 (leased, fourteen hundred a month), the Lexus detail may be more thematically functional. If so, change Ch1/7 'Audi' to 'Lexus.'

findings.acceptance_test: The RT envelope has a consistent size and seal state across Ch8 and Ch11.
findings.evidence: Ch8/2026: describes the RT envelope as 'the legal-size envelope' with 'a metal clasp that had been bent open and closed often enough to loosen it.' Ch11/2739: 'A sealed manila envelope, letter-sized, with the letters RT written on it.' Ch8 says the clasp was loose from repeated opening; Ch11 says the envelope is 'sealed' with 'flap unbroken' (line 2773).
findings.problem: The RT envelope is 'legal-size' with a loose, oft-opened clasp in Ch8, but 'letter-sized' and 'sealed' with 'flap unbroken' in Ch11. The physical state of the envelope contradicts between chapters — it cannot be both repeatedly opened and sealed/unbroken.
findings.rewrite_direction: Decide the canonical state: was the envelope previously opened (Ch8's loose clasp) or sealed (Ch11's unbroken flap)? If Cliff opened it in Ch8, Paul should find it re-closed but not sealed. Reconcile size to one designation.

findings.acceptance_test: The day the fire occurs is consistent between the two renderings of Martin's document. Tommy's memory in Ch12 either matches or is flagged as uncertain.
findings.evidence: Ch8/2048: Tommy had been 'drinking since they'd left Minneapolis.' Ch16/4242: 'Tommy was drinking heavy on Saturday, worse than usual, and usual was already a lot. Around 9 PM he went outside to smoke.' Ch12/2972: 'Friday night. Bonfire. Too much lighter fluid.'
findings.problem: The fire document in Ch8 says they arrived and the fire happened the same night (Tommy drinking 'since they'd left Minneapolis'). The Ch16 version says they'd been there 'since Friday' and the fire happened Saturday night. Ch12 Tommy's memory says 'Friday night.' The day of the fire is inconsistent across all three versions.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile as part of the fire-document reconciliation (C01). The document should specify one consistent day. Tommy's memory in Ch12 may diverge intentionally but should not contradict the document on basic timeline.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
